title: Rudrakodisvarar Temple, Sempankudi
tagline: Rudrakodisvarar Temple, Sempankudi — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Rudrakodisvarar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located at Sempankudi in Mayiladuthurai district in Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: Rudrakodisvarar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located at Sempankudi in Mayiladuthurai district in Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Velladainatha Swami Temple
topPlaces.description: Velladainatha Swami Temple is a Hindu temple situated in the village of Thirukkarukavur in the Mayiladuthurai district of Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Sirkazhi
topPlaces.description: Sirkazhi ([ˈsiːɾkaːɻiː]), also spelled as Sirkali and Siyali, is a selection grade municipal town in Mayiladuthurai district in Tamil Nadu, India. It is located 13 km (8 mi) from the coast of the Bay of Bengal, and 250 km (160 mi) from the state capital Chennai.

topPlaces.name: Kazheesirama Vinnagaram
topPlaces.description: Kazheesirama Vinnagaram or Tadalan Kovil or Tiruvikrama (trivikara) Perumal Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu located in Sirkazhi, of Mayiladuthurai district, Tamil Nadu, India. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desam dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Trivikrama and his consort Lakshmi as Loganayagi.

topPlaces.name: Sattainathar Temple, Sirkazhi
topPlaces.description: Sattainathar temple, Sirkazhi (also called Brahmapureeswarar temple and Thoniappar temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva located in Sirkali, Mayiladuthurai district, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is incarnated by the hymns of Thevaram and is classified as Paadal Petra Sthalam.

topPlaces.name: Thiruvali-Thirunagari
topPlaces.description: The Thiruvali - Thirunagari Temples are paired Hindu temples dedicated to Vishnu located 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) from Sirkali in Tamil Nadu, India, and 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) from each other. It is one of the Divya Desams, the 108 temples of Vishnu revered by the 12 poet saints, or Alvars.

topPlaces.name: Vedarajan temple, Thirunagari
topPlaces.description: Vedarajan Temple in Thirunagari, a village in Mayiladuthurai district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Naalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Vedarajan and his consort Lakshmi as Amruthavalli.

topPlaces.name: Agani
topPlaces.description: Agani is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Sakleshpur taluk of Hassan district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Azhagiyasingar Temple, Thiruvali
topPlaces.description: The Alagiyasingar Temple in Thiruvali, a village in Mayiladuthurai district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Alagiyasingar and his consort Lakshmi as Purnavalli.

Sirkazhi ([ˈsiːɾkaːɻiː]), also spelled as Sirkali and Siyali, is a selection grade municipal town in Mayiladuthurai district in Tamil Nadu, India. It is located 13 km (8 mi) from the coast of the Bay of Bengal, and 250 km (160 mi) from the state capital Chennai.

Kazheesirama Vinnagaram or Tadalan Kovil or Tiruvikrama (trivikara) Perumal Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu located in Sirkazhi, of Mayiladuthurai district, Tamil Nadu, India. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. Sattainathar temple, Sirkazhi (also called Brahmapureeswarar temple and Thoniappar temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva located in Sirkali, Mayiladuthurai district, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is incarnated by the hymns of Thevaram and is classified as Paadal Petra Sthalam.

The Thiruvali - Thirunagari Temples are paired Hindu temples dedicated to Vishnu located 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) from Sirkali in Tamil Nadu, India, and 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) from each other. It is one of the Divya Desams, the 108 temples of Vishnu revered by the 12 poet saints, or Alvars.

Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Naalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Vedarajan and his consort Lakshmi as Amruthavalli.
Agani is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Sakleshpur taluk of Hassan district in Karnataka.

The Alagiyasingar Temple in Thiruvali, a village in Mayiladuthurai district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Alagiyasingar and his consort Lakshmi as Purnavalli.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Rudrakodisvarar Temple, Sempankudi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Rudrakodisvarar Temple, Sempankudi is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Rudrakodisvarar Temple, Sempankudi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (127 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (127 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Rudrakodisvarar Temple, Sempankudi?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Rudrakodisvarar Temple, Sempankudi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Rudrakodisvarar Temple, Sempankudi?
faq.a: Stays in Rudrakodisvarar Temple, Sempankudi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Rudrakodisvarar Temple, Sempankudi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Velladainatha Swami Temple, Sirkazhi, Kazheesirama Vinnagaram, Sattainathar Temple, Sirkazhi, Thiruvali-Thirunagari.
